title: Bruce the Parrot: A Bird Who Overcame a Challenge
summary: Bruce is a kea parrot from New Zealand who lost his upper beak in an accident when he was young. Even without his full beak, Bruce became the top bird in his group at a wildlife reserve. He uses a special fighting style called beak-jousting to win fights against other male keas. Bruce also figured out how to clean his feathers with small pebbles, showing he is very smart. Scientists studied Bruce and found that he is not stressed and gets to eat first because he is the leader. His story teaches us that animals can find new ways to do things even when they have a disability, and sometimes they don't need extra help like fake beaks.

keywords.explanation: a type of parrot that lives in New Zealand
a fighting method where Bruce uses his half-beak to push other birds
being the leader or top bird in a group
small stones that Bruce uses to clean his feathers
when an animal has a physical problem, like missing a beak

options: Animals can find new ways with disabilities
Only strong animals can be leaders
questions.correct_answer: Animals can find new ways with disabilities

background_read: Kea parrots are smart birds from New Zealand that live in groups called circuses. They are known for being curious and good at solving problems. In the wild, keas use their beaks to eat, play, and fight. Bruce's story shows how animals can adapt when they face challenges, like losing a body part. Scientists study animals like Bruce to learn more about how they think and behave, which helps us understand nature better.
Article_Structure: WHO: Bruce, a kea parrot from New Zealand
WHAT: He became the leader in his group despite missing his upper beak by using beak-jousting and pebbles for cleaning
WHERE: At the Willowbank Wildlife Reserve in New Zealand
WHY: To show that animals with disabilities can innovate and succeed without always needing human help

perspectives.description: Bruce shows how animals can change their behavior to overcome physical challenges.
